seoTitle: How to Redirect Non-WWW to WWW in Cloudflare
metaDescription: Set up a permanent 301 redirect from your root domain to www using Cloudflare's Redirect Rules and a proxied placeholder DNS record.
intro: Consolidating traffic to a single version of your domain, either root or www, helps avoid duplicate content issues and keeps links consistent. This guide covers setting up a proxied placeholder record and building the redirect with Cloudflare's Rules engine.

steps.heading: Add a placeholder A record
steps.body: In DNS Records, add a proxied dummy A record for the apex root symbol '@' using the placeholder IP 192.0.2.1, which forces traffic through Cloudflare's edge rules.

steps.heading: Open the Rules directory
steps.body: Navigate to the Rules section in the sidebar and select Redirect Rules.

steps.heading: Choose a redirect template
steps.body: Click Create Rule and select the pre-made 'Redirect from Root to WWW' configuration template to auto-fill the required expressions.

steps.heading: Review the redirect settings
steps.body: Check the filter expression and forwarding destination, then set the status to 301 Permanent Redirect and enable query string preservation.

steps.heading: Deploy and test
steps.body: Click Deploy to activate the rule, then run a live access test to confirm requests to your root domain now forward correctly to www.

faq.question: Why do I need a placeholder A record for this redirect to work?
faq.answer: Cloudflare's Redirect Rules only apply to proxied traffic, so the apex domain needs an active proxied record to route through the edge rules engine in the first place.

faq.question: What's the significance of the 192.0.2.1 placeholder IP?
faq.answer: It's a reserved documentation IP address that doesn't route anywhere real, used purely to satisfy the requirement for a proxied record without pointing to an actual server.

faq.question: Should I use a 301 or a 302 redirect?
faq.answer: A 301 Permanent Redirect is generally recommended for a root-to-www setup since it signals to search engines that the change is permanent.

faq.question: Will this redirect preserve query strings and specific page paths?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you enable the query string preservation option when configuring the redirect rule.
